meta.fallback: Theme collection variables RESTORED to alias-only architecture (Batch 6 Phase 4 V7). All semantic theme vars now reference palette tokens via VARIABLE_ALIAS; zero raw HEX in theme. Earlier Batches 1+4 had stripped aliases by using setValueForMode with raw RGB; that violation is now reverted.
meta.notes: Dual-temperature brand: warm metallic gold (palette.brand.500=#c49557) for chrome/CTAs, deep brand-teal (palette.neutral.500=#3a7d7d) for neutral surfaces. text/neutral/* aliased to palette.brand.* warm ramp due to impedance mismatch (text wanted warm beige, palette.neutral is teal). All theme-collection vars use VARIABLE_ALIAS — zero raw HEX. text/brand/base dual-character: aliased to palette.neutral.475 (teal) per doctrine. ACCENT FIX: main/accent/* re-aliased from legacy lime palette/accent/* to palette/neutral/* (the canonical teal family). Accent and neutral now share the unified teal brand core; accent uses brighter intensity steps.

l200._note: TEAL — accent shares the teal color space with neutral, not the legacy lime palette/accent ramp

base._note: bright teal — closest match to Reference's main/accent/base #319f9f

50._note: warm-beige impedance: aliased to brand ramp (warm) since palette.neutral is teal

base._note: DUAL-CHARACTER: text/brand is teal (matches neutral palette), not gold

themeDark._status: untouched in V7 alias restoration; reflects previous setValueForMode raw values; needs same alias treatment if dark mode is activated

aliases.main/brand/contrast: #fbf8f4 (concept warm cream — overrides default neutral/975 alias)

proposals.rationale: Embossed legibility on the metallic-plate primary surface; matches brand tactile-3D character.

stops._note: TODO: Resolve remote figma token link — placeholder
TODO: Resolve remote figma token link — placeholder

proposals.rationale: Sibling stop in the brand-bezel highlight family.

blendMode._note: Figma Plugin API rejects PLUS_LIGHTER; LINEAR_DODGE is the closest additive equivalent. Visual delta minor at 15% opacity.

proposals.rationale: Metallic-plate brand surface; defines tactile finish distinguishing primary from secondary.
Centerpiece chrome detail; coupled to bezel-cap aesthetic.
Warm off-white at the lightest text-neutral end; required for full Reference typography fidelity. Created in active theme collection in Phase 4.
Reference uses Jost across all type ramps; the active theme had Inter with letter-spacing -0.25. Overwriting brings type rendering into Reference fidelity. File-wide impact.

value.retractionReason: Phase 4 swapped 4 MainBanner.Button instances from local set 4050:45382 to remote-flagged set 168369:46800 (remote:true; imported from an unsubscribed library). User flagged the regression: the swap (a) pointed instances at foreign assets not owned by the file, (b) didn't propagate to adjacent layouts (instance-level swap doesn't update siblings), (c) broke label rendering — the new remote set's TEXT node wasn't wired to the carried label property, so 'Join Now' rendered as 'Label'. Path A rollback executed: all 4 instances swapped back to local 4050:45382; labels re-set and verified rendering 'Join Now'.

proposals.rationale: Doctrine: never swap instances to remote-flagged components. See Rule 8 in forgex_architectural_doctrine.md.

value.chromeStructure: Horizontal auto-layout: left-cap-slot(14 FIXED, clip) | left-strip(FILL, gradient) | center-cap-slot(32 FIXED, clip) | right-strip(FILL, gradient) | right-cap-slot(14 FIXED, clip mirrored). Texture overlay absolute-positioned with LINEAR_DODGE blend, opacity 0.15. Vertical layoutSizing FILL on all children so chrome adapts to parent height.
value.extendedSet: 4050:45382 (MainBanner.Button) — added style axis (plain|branded). 9 existing variants renamed to append style=plain. 3 new branded variants created (size={lg,md,sm}, state=default, style=branded) each containing BackgroundChrome instance absolute-positioned to fill parent dimensions.

usedBy: BaseEntrancePage/Unlogged JOIN NOW (4 instances across mobile/tablet/laptop/desktop breakpoints)
proposals.rationale: Replaces the rejected remote-swap approach (see mainbanner-button-branded-set-RETRACTED). Local-owned chrome COMPONENT + clean style=branded variant axis on the canonical local set. Preserves existing instances (default style=plain), Code Connect remains intact, no remote dependency.

value.final_state: ZERO raw HEX in theme; 112 aliases + 1 non-color var
value.impedance_mismatch_documented: text/neutral/* aliased to palette.brand.* (warm) as best-fit since palette.neutral is teal but Reference wanted warm beige for text neutrals
value.verification_script: doctrine/verify-button-colors.js (extended to validate alias presence, not just visual pixels)

proposals.rationale: Restoration of broken alias chain. Earlier Batches 1+4 setValueForMode raw RGB stripped pre-existing VARIABLE_ALIAS. This proposal documents the restoration. Going forward: never call setValueForMode with raw RGB on a semantic-theme variable; always use createVariableAlias().

value.before: main/accent/* → palette/accent/* (lime green #7fc03d base — pre-ForgeX, never updated)
value.after: main/accent/* → palette/neutral/* teal steps (l200→300, l100→400, base→475, d100→625, d200→750, contrast→25)
value.rationale: Reference confirms accent and neutral share the same teal color space; accent is brighter, neutral is darker, both pull from palette/neutral/* at different intensity steps
value.palette_accent_remains: untouched (lime values still there for any consumer that needs them; theme just no longer references them)

proposals.rationale: Earlier V7 alias restoration mapped main/accent to palette/accent without verifying the palette/accent ramp was lime green. User identified the brand-logic mismatch — accent should be teal per Reference. Fixed by re-aliasing to palette/neutral/*.

proposals.tokenName: BackgroundChrome (local COMPONENT_SET) + MainBanner.Button branded style axis

verification.target_join_now_buttons: 4 instances resolve to size-matched branded chrome (sm 192x48, 2x md 220-224x56, lg 247x72)

proposals.rationale: V8 used a single Chrome component with math-scaled cap widths (40→27) to fit md/sm sizes. User rejected because source 168407:121243 has 9 dedicated Component 1 variants — each with its own pre-baked image hashes and per-size proportioned chrome (defaults use different base hashes between lg and md/sm; hover/pressed add highlight+shadow overlay layers absent from defaults). V9 replicates that 1:1 matrix as 9 local COMPONENTs in a BackgroundChrome COMPONENT_SET. Each branded MainBanner.Button variant instances the matching size+state chrome. Math-scaling is no longer used.
